opening_query: I am seeking a consultation for a lawyer in regards to injuries I got on the job that I feel were preventable if my supervisor had listened to me. It wasn't a sudden onset injury but developed over time, I went to my lead when it became obvious something was wrong. He blew it off saying I was just tired. After a couple months I went back to him again complaining of pain, he blew it off. Over 18 months I went to him 5 times before he told me I needed a doctor's note, I went to the doctor the next day and was immediately put on light duty. My supervisor tried saying that I didn't come to him "officially" but there's no "official" procedure. There's a giant sign in the breakroom saying "if you are injured, no matter small, tell your supervisor." Eventually I was moved to another department and after 30 days was laid off with 43 other people. I have had 2 surgeries, with another on the way. Am in constant pain, have limitations on my primary arm and more. Thank you

I am being garnished from Oregon Department of Unemployment for an overpayment of over $20,000 with a 1% interest added on each month. I had requested their hardship application and submitted the documents via email within the timeframe I was given. I was later called and told that I would be garnished under the pretense that when I submitted the documents, I did not follow the instructions for I needed to submit the documents And Call. She said that there was a note on her end that said I was to call by the women I spoke to who sent me the hardship application. I don’t remember the conversation that well but I don’t remember her saying I need to call. On the email she sent me, nothing says to call, I have read it over numerous times. If this was such an important step that determines garnishment or not, why wasn’t it stressed to the fullest? And only apparently verbally said. I felt like there was nothing I could do over the phone discussing this with the person I talked to. I don’t know how to navigate any of this. She even told me the amount that I would be paying each month and what they considered in their final decision and what was not considered in their final decision. Even telling me about the waiver for forgiveness of the overpayment that I submitted and how it was unlikely that it was going to get granted due to the category that it was put into. But all of that didn’t matter because I didn’t call. I’m 25 years old with other bills and living expenses, I’m not the best with finances and already have a lot of debt that I struggle to pay and I’m being asked to pay back money that I don’t have anymore that was given to me over the course of about 11 months during this pandemic with interest. I would like to request a lawyer who can help me with getting out of the garnishment.

merged_screen.question: Is your problem about unemployment benefits overpayment and wage garnishment?
options: Yes — unemployment overpayment with wage garnishment

merged_screen.question: Have you gotten a written decision or notice about hardship or a waiver, and does it list an appeal deadline?
Do you have documents showing you submitted the hardship application (emails or forms)?

On that day she asked if she should live at home anymore. I said no, not anymore. My siblings and I found an apartment in an independent living community, and chose an apartment. We took her to the apartment to see it and she discussed what furnishings and decorating ect. She was hesitant but feeling good my the end of the meal we had there. opening_query: I have a case with the Oregon Board of Licensed Professional Counselors and Therapists in which I have been found guilty. Theee tree are several problems with which the Board handled the case. For example, one allegation was made , as written in the initial “request acknowledgment “ where the claim states that I referred the client to my uncle for an assessment. When I showed that this was not possible, since my uncle died two years prior, the investigator said in a recorded phone call that she did not need my response or to discuss it because it “was just a misunderstanding.” When I received the legal documents outlining the allegations and sanctions, the allegation had changed to I made her feel uncomfortable and confused by going on and on about my uncle. It is clear that once I could prove the allegation was untrue, the client’s story changed. The Board should have noted from there that she is an unreliable reporter. However, there were several other allegations that the Board took as true. The type that I cannot provide proof I did not do or say. But again, the way the investigator presented the allegations, I could not answer because I could not recall such generalities. When given the legal documents with details, I can say that I did not, and would not have done such things. I want to request a hearing and need advice as to whether I should have an attorney represent me, and if there is any point to pursuing a hearing based on the case information.

merged_screen.question: What is the current status of your Board case?
options: I got a final order with sanctions
I got a proposed order or notice of intent and I can still ask for a hearing
I’m not sure which one it is

merged_screen.question: What discipline or penalties does the papers list for you?
Do you want to challenge the Board’s decision, and if so, on what grounds?
options: Yes — I think the decision was unfair (found guilty unfairly)
Yes — I think the process wasn’t done correctly or rules weren’t followed (due process / rules not followed)
Yes — both unfair and due process issue

opening_query: I have just received news that I am to be "laid off" from my staff Pharmacist position at Cascada because another pharmacist was chosen to be Pharmacist in Charge (PIC) over myself, due to an updated Oregon Board of Pharmacy requirement. My hours have been taken from me to give to the new PIC because of the Board requirement; however, the new Board requirement does not specify how many hours a PIC must be in the pharmacy, only that "a pharmacy must, at all times have one Pharmacist-in-Charge(PIC) who is normally present in the pharmacy on a regular basis." There is another pharmacist who staffs the same pharmacy I do two times a week and her hours have not been affected, only mine and we are both full time employees. I was hired before this other pharmacist. I was told that the decision was not made on performance and that I have completed my job responsibilities and duties far beyond the standard. I do not believe that my laying off is consistent through Cascada.
